title: Azure Angel II

{{Quote|You know the modifications I made—and you showed me a few tricks yourself. I know I can outmaneuver anything the Rendili put down.|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] to [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]]|<ref name="DoR">''[[Star Wars: Republic: Dreadnaughts of Rendili]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Azure Angel II''''' was a modified [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7 ''Aethersprite''-class light interceptor]] piloted by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==History==
After the original ''[[Azure Angel]]'' was [[Duel on Yavin 4|destroyed]] by [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]],<ref name="CW17">{{CW|17}}</ref> Anakin personally customized a new one to suit his tastes and, like the first, named it in honor of his secret wife, [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]].

The ship was nearly lost in a space [[Battle of Praesitlyn|battle]] above [[Praesitlyn/Legends|Praesitlyn]], in which it defeated several [[Droid starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]], an enemy [[Destroyer/Legends|destroyer]], and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] ''[[Corpulentus]]''. It also participated in the [[Battle of Rendili]], disabling the command [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruiser]] in the [[Rendili/Legends|Rendili]] fleet. It also came equipped with a very light shield generator and its own [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], meaning it did not require a [[Hyperspace transport ring/Legends|hyperdrive ring]].

As of the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]], it is thought this starfighter may have been stationed on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Azure Angel II'', or an identical starfighter, is visible on a Coruscant landing pad in one of the levels of the [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|''Revenge of the Sith'' video game]], albeit only in the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo DS|Nintendo DS]] and [[Wikipedia:Game Boy Advance|Game Boy Advance]] versions. This suggests that it may have survived at least until the end of the Clone Wars.
